jobText:
Frontend Developer
 віддалено до $2000
Requirements
Proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
Strong experience with TypeScript (interfaces, types, and generics)
Deep knowledge of React.js and its core principles (Hooks, Context API)
Hands-on experience with Next.js (App Router, Server Components, SSR/SSG)
Proficiency in state management libraries (Redux Toolkit, Zustand, or MobX)
Familiarity with UI component libraries (shadcn/ui, Radix UI, MUI, etc)
Knowledge of Web Performance optimization and Web Vitals
Experience with Unit and Integration testing (Jest, Vitest, React Testing Library)
Responsibilities
Develop high-quality, scalable web applications using React.js and Next.js
Translate Figma designs and wireframes into high-quality, pixel-perfect, and responsive code
Build reusable, modular components and front-end libraries for cross-project use
Optimize applications for maximum speed, scalability, and optimal Core Web Vitals
Implement Server-Side Rendering (SSR) and Static Site Generation (SSG) to improve SEO and performance
Collaborate with UI/UX designers to ensure the technical feasibility of designs
Intervew steps
pre-screen.
tech interview.

must_have: Proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
Strong experience with TypeScript (interfaces, types, and generics)
Deep knowledge of React.js and its core principles (Hooks, Context API)
Hands-on experience with Next.js (App Router, Server Components, SSR/SSG)
Proficiency in state management libraries (Redux Toolkit, Zustand, or MobX)
Experience with Unit and Integration testing (Jest, Vitest, React Testing Library)
nice_to_have: Familiarity with UI component libraries (shadcn/ui, Radix UI, MUI, etc)
Knowledge of Web Performance optimization and Web Vitals
responsibilities: Develop high-quality, scalable web applications using React.js and Next.js
Translate Figma designs and wireframes into high-quality, pixel-perfect, and responsive code
Build reusable, modular components and front-end libraries for cross-project use
Optimize applications for maximum speed, scalability, and optimal Core Web Vitals
Implement Server-Side Rendering (SSR) and Static Site Generation (SSG) to improve SEO and performance
Collaborate with UI/UX designers to ensure the technical feasibility of designs
red_flags: Salary capped at $2000 — may be below market for the depth of TypeScript, Next.js, and testing expertise required
No years of experience specified, making seniority level ambiguous
No mention of team size, product context, or company name
Very short interview process description with no detail on tech interview format

tailored.headline: Frontend Developer | React.js, Next.js, TypeScript
tailored.summary: Frontend Developer with 5+ years of hands-on experience building scalable web applications using React.js, Next.js, and TypeScript. Comfortable owning features end-to-end — from translating Figma designs into pixel-perfect, responsive interfaces to integrating REST and GraphQL APIs. Experienced with SSR/SSG, state management (Redux Toolkit, Zustand), and writing reliable Unit and Integration tests with Jest, Vitest, and React Testing Library.

bullets: Designed and delivered a monorepo architecture using Nx to unify 3 products (marketplace, admin dashboard, landing pages), maximizing reusable component and business logic sharing across the ecosystem

Owned the development of core marketplace features — secure authentication flows, a multi-step order creation engine, and an advanced search UI with dynamic filtering — serving 50K+ monthly active users
Built a real-time P2P communication layer using WebSockets, enabling live chat messaging and transaction status synchronization between buyers and sellers
Delivered a full-featured admin panel with financial dashboards, role-based access control (RBAC), subscription management, and dispute resolution tools
Developed SEO-optimized marketing landing pages with Next.js (SSR/SSG), integrating analytics and A/B testing configurations that improved user conversion rates by ~15%
Collaborated closely with backend engineers, designers, and PMs to break down requirements into scalable, well-scoped frontend tasks
Configured CI/CD pipelines, production monitoring, and code review workflows to maintain high engineering standards across the team
Designed and shipped a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js (SSG), improving page speed scores and organic search visibility for B2B user acquisition
Owned the stabilization of an existing React.js admin dashboard — conducted a code audit, fixed critical bugs in business logic, broken API requests, and improper error handling
Implemented a subscription and billing system via Stripe integration, covering multi-tier pricing pages, billing management UI, and transaction status workflows
Developed an isolated embeddable chat widget using Shadow DOM and WebSockets, ensuring clean DOM containment and real-time AI response delivery on third-party client websites
Built an Integrations Hub enabling users to connect Slack, Telegram, and Webhooks to route chatbot leads and notifications to external platforms
Refactored the data-fetching layer with TanStack Query, establishing robust caching strategies, background revalidation, and consistent loading/error state handling
Optimized Core Web Vitals (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing bundle size by ~10% and improving widget load times by ~15%
Integrated Sentry for error tracking and production monitoring, significantly reducing mean time to debug and improving application observability
Contributed to building and launching the platform from scratch, delivering features across the admin panel and marketing modules in a fast-paced startup environment
Built a pixel-perfect, SEO-optimized marketing landing page with Next.js using semantic HTML5, custom meta tags, and fully responsive CSS3 layouts
Developed an interactive Widget Builder (Alert Box, Goal Progress) allowing streamers to customize colors, typography, animations, and sounds for OBS overlays
Implemented OAuth integrations with Twitch, YouTube, and Kick for streamer authorization, profile sync, and live broadcast status tracking
Built data-heavy analytical dashboards with responsive data tables (1000+ rows), pagination, filtering, and interactive financial charts for real-time revenue tracking
Designed multi-step configuration forms with advanced validation for donation thresholds, media file constraints, and profanity filtering
Collaborated with backend engineers to integrate REST APIs and WebSockets, managing global state with Redux for reliable, real-time alert distribution
Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma designs using modular, maintainable SCSS, ensuring cross-browser compatibility across all device sizes

coverage.requirement: Proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
coverage.evidence: Used across all 3 roles; pixel-perfect layouts, semantic HTML, responsive CSS3, modern JS patterns throughout

coverage.requirement: Strong experience with TypeScript (interfaces, types, and generics)
coverage.evidence: TypeScript listed as primary language across all roles and all tech stacks (KYC Market, AMO FINTECH, DonateSystem)

coverage.requirement: Deep knowledge of React.js and its core principles (Hooks, Context API)
coverage.evidence: React.js is the core framework across all 3 roles spanning 4+ years; complex UI systems built with hooks-based architecture

coverage.requirement: Hands-on experience with Next.js (App Router, Server Components, SSR/SSG)
coverage.evidence: Next.js used at KYC Market, AMO FINTECH, and DonateSystem for SSR/SSG landing pages and SEO-optimized apps

coverage.requirement: Proficiency in state management libraries (Redux Toolkit, Zustand, or MobX)
coverage.evidence: Zustand used at KYC Market; Redux used at DonateSystem; TanStack Query and Apollo Client at AMO FINTECH

coverage.requirement: Experience with Unit and Integration testing (Jest, Vitest, React Testing Library)
coverage.evidence: Jest, Vitest, and React Testing Library listed in tech stack at KYC Market and AMO FINTECH; Playwright also used

coverage.requirement: Familiarity with UI component libraries (shadcn/ui, Radix UI, MUI, etc)
coverage.evidence: Material UI used at AMO FINTECH; no shadcn/ui or Radix UI mentioned in master CV

coverage.requirement: Knowledge of Web Performance optimization and Web Vitals
coverage.evidence: Optimized LCP and TTI at AMO FINTECH (~10% bundle reduction, ~15% widget load improvement); A/B testing and analytics at KYC Market

coverage.requirement: Translate Figma designs into pixel-perfect, responsive code
coverage.evidence: Explicitly mentioned at DonateSystem: pixel-perfect layouts from Figma using SCSS, cross-browser compatibility

coverage.requirement: Build reusable, modular components for cross-project use
coverage.evidence: Monorepo at KYC Market built specifically to maximize UI component and business logic reuse across 3 products

coverage.requirement: Implement SSR and SSG to improve SEO and performance
coverage.evidence: Next.js SSR/SSG used at KYC Market and AMO FINTECH for SEO-optimized landing pages; semantic HTML and meta tags at DonateSystem
Pixel-perfect Figma implementation at DonateSystem; collaboration with designers mentioned at KYC Market

tailored.match_notes: Kostya is a very strong match for this role technically — every must-have is covered with real, hands-on evidence across multiple projects. The main honest gap is shadcn/ui and Radix UI, which are listed as nice-to-have; MUI experience partially covers this. The role is positioned as middle-level, and the CV has been reframed accordingly to highlight feature ownership, cross-team collaboration, and delivery quality rather than team leadership or architectural strategy. One real-world consideration: Kostya's actual experience is senior-level, so the $2,000 salary cap flagged in the job analysis may be a point of friction worth evaluating before applying.

keyword_coverage.location: headline, summary, top_skills, AMO FINTECH bullet 2
headline, summary, top_skills, KYC Market bullet 5, AMO FINTECH bullet 1, DonateSystem bullet 2
summary, top_skills, KYC Market bullet 5, AMO FINTECH bullet 1
summary, top_skills, KYC Market bullet 5, AMO FINTECH bullet 1
top_skills, KYC Market bullet 5, AMO FINTECH bullet 1, DonateSystem bullet 2
summary (scalable web applications), KYC Market bullet 6

format_checks.rule: All experience bullets start with an action verb
format_checks.note: KYC Market bullet 1 starts with 'Designed' ✓, but bullet 2 starts with 'Owned' — while technically a verb, 'Owned' is borderline and may not be parsed as an action verb by all ATS engines. More critically, DonateSystem bullet 1 starts with 'Contributed to building' which is a weak, passive-adjacent construction. All other bullets pass.

format_checks.rule: No passive constructions (Responsible for / Helped with)
format_checks.note: DonateSystem bullet 1: 'Contributed to building and launching the platform' is a passive-adjacent phrase that dilutes impact and may be flagged. No 'Responsible for' or 'Helped with' found, but 'Contributed to' carries the same ATS risk.

format_checks.rule: Standard sections present (headline, summary, skills, experience)
TypeScript depth keywords present (interfaces, types, generics)
format_checks.note: TypeScript is listed as a top skill and appears in the headline, but the specific sub-keywords 'interfaces', 'types', and 'generics' — which appear verbatim in the job's ATS keyword list — are absent from all sections of the CV.

format_checks.rule: React.js depth keywords present (Hooks, Context API)
format_checks.note: 'Hooks' and 'Context API' are explicitly listed ATS keywords but do not appear anywhere in the CV. React.js is mentioned, but ATS systems scan for these terms independently.

format_checks.rule: Next.js depth keywords present (App Router, Server Components)
format_checks.note: 'App Router' and 'Server Components' are absent from all sections. SSR/SSG are covered, but these Next.js-specific terms are separate ATS targets.

format_checks.rule: Full-form SSR/SSG synonyms present (Server-Side Rendering, Static Site Generation)
format_checks.note: Only abbreviations 'SSR' and 'SSG' are used. The job's ATS keyword list includes both the abbreviations AND the full forms 'Server-Side Rendering' and 'Static Site Generation' as separate entries. At least one full-form mention is missing.

format_checks.rule: UI library keywords present (MUI, shadcn/ui, Radix UI)
format_checks.note: The CV lists 'Material UI' in top_skills but the ATS keyword is 'MUI' — a partial match risk. 'shadcn/ui' and 'Radix UI' are entirely absent. Even a brief mention as 'familiar with' in the skills section would capture these keywords.
